{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/24734785?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/f1veT/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/f1veT/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/f1veT/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/f1veT/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/f1veT", "id": 24734785, "login": "f1veT", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjI0NzM0Nzg1", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/f1veT/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/f1veT/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/f1veT/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/f1veT/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/f1veT/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/f1veT", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-54250", "cvss": {"score": 5.8, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:L/PR:H/UI:R/S:U/C:N/I:H/A:H"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 5.8, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:L/PR:H/UI:R/S:U/C:N/I:H/A:H"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-22", "name": "Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')"}], "description": "#### Summary

A path traversal vulnerability exists in K3s's etcd snapshot decompression functionality. Zip files containing archive members with maliciously crafted names (e.g., `../../../../etc/password`) can be written to arbitrary locations on the filesystem when an administrator restores the archive as a compressed etcd snapshot.

#### Mitigations

* Enable golang's built-in [insecure path protections](https://pkg.go.dev/archive/zip#NewReader) when restoring snapshots by setting the`GODEBUG` environment variable:
    ```bash
    GODEBUG=zipinsecurepath=0 k3s server --cluster-reset --cluster-reset-restore-path=/path/to/snapshot.zip
    ```
* Manually extract the snapshot from the zip archive before restoring it. If the snapshot to be restored does not end with `.zip`, the vulnerable extraction code will not be executed.

#### Additional Notes

Administrators should be aware of the cautions noted in the "Security" section of the documentation on [Restoring Snapshots](https://docs.k3s.io/cli/etcd-snapshot#security).", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00122, "percentile": 0.0229}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-jxr7-mqhw-9p98", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-07-14T17:54:14Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-jxr7-mqhw-9p98", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-jxr7-mqhw-9p98"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-54250"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-06-25T19:16:41Z", "published_at": "2026-07-14T17:54:14Z", "references": ["https://github.com/k3s-io/k3s/security/advisories/GHSA-jxr7-mqhw-9p98", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-54250", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-jxr7-mqhw-9p98"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/k3s-io/k3s/security-advisories/GHSA-jxr7-mqhw-9p98", "severity": "medium", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/k3s-io/k3s", "summary": "K3s: ZIP Archive Path Traversal Vulnerability in etcd Snapshot Decompression", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-07-14T17:54:15Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-jxr7-mqhw-9p98", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "1.35.3", "package": {"ecosystem": "go", "name": "github.com/k3s-io/k3s"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 1.35.0-rc1, < 1.35.3"}, {"first_patched_version": "1.34.6", "package": {"ecosystem": "go", "name": "github.com/k3s-io/k3s"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 1.34.0-rc1, < 1.34.6"}, {"first_patched_version": "1.33.10", "package": {"ecosystem": "go", "name": "github.com/k3s-io/k3s"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 1.33.10"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

reasons: 資産不一致または判定情報不足

title: K3s: ZIP Archive Path Traversal Vulnerability in etcd Snapshot Decompression
